cards.title: Anthropic Joins Frontier Carbon Removal Coalition
cards.body: Anthropic has become the first AI startup to join the Frontier coalition, which received $915M in new pledges for carbon removal projects. The move signals AI companies' commitment to addressing environmental impact.

cards.title: Social Media Platforms Enable User-Controlled Algorithms
cards.body: Threads, Instagram, and TikTok are introducing tools that let users directly influence their recommendation algorithms. The shift marks a major move toward customizable social media experiences.

cards.title: Odyssey Reaches $1.45B Valuation on World Models
cards.body: World model startup Odyssey secured funding backed by Amazon and other major investors at a $1.45B valuation. The company is emerging as a key player in AI development beyond large language models.

cards.title: Americans Skeptical About AI's Societal Impact
cards.body: Only 16% of Americans believe AI will have a positive impact on society, according to a Pew Research study. Public sentiment contrasts sharply with Wall Street's enthusiasm for the technology.

cards.title: Google Launches Gemini-Powered Smart Speaker
cards.body: Google's new $99.99 Home Speaker replaces rigid voice commands with conversational Gemini interactions. The launch represents Google's bet on generative AI to revitalize the smart speaker market.

cards.title: Slowtech Movement Gains Momentum Amid Phone Addiction
cards.body: A new slowtech revolution is emerging as consumers seek to reclaim control over their attention and time. The movement challenges the always-on digital lifestyle with tools designed for disconnection.

cards.title: AI Labs Outsource Physical Training Data Collection
cards.body: Some AI research labs are contracting with XDOF to collect robot training data, a unglamorous but critical task. Solving physical AI's data problem is essential for matching LLM-level accomplishments.

cards.title: Pramaana Labs Raises $27M for AI Formal Verification
cards.body: Pramaana Labs secured $27M in seed funding from Khosla Ventures to bring formal verification to AI systems. The startup will target sensitive sectors like law, drug discovery, and tax preparation.

cards.title: Canadian Pension Giant Invests in India Data Centers
cards.body: A major Canadian pension fund is acquiring an 8.2% stake in CtrlS, which operates 15+ data centers across India. The investment highlights growing competition to fund Asia's AI infrastructure boom.

cards.title: DeepL Acquires Mixhalo for Live Event Translation
cards.body: Translation platform DeepL acquired Mixhalo to expand into live-event audio streaming and translation services. The deal includes a new San Francisco office as DeepL grows its U.S. presence.

cards.title: Pinterest Launches Experimental AI Shopping App
cards.body: Pinterest introduced 'Ask Pinterest,' an experimental AI-powered shopping app offering conversational recommendations. The app lets users seek product inspiration through natural language interactions.

cards.title: Anthropic Gains Market Share Amid Trump Admin Tensions
cards.body: Business user adoption of Anthropic's products continues to surge despite recent conflicts with the Trump administration. Sales data from Ramp suggests the controversy may actually boost Anthropic's growth.

cards.title: SpaceX Valuation Surges to $2.6 Trillion Post-IPO
cards.body: SpaceX's valuation jumped by $1 trillion following the launch of public share trading on Friday. The company briefly surpassed Amazon in market value following the historic milestone.

cards.title: Android 17 Expands Gemini AI Features Across Devices
cards.body: Google released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with new multitasking, parental controls, and security tools. The Pixel Drop brings Google's latest AI models directly to its hardware ecosystem.

cards.title: 60% of US Consumers Turned Off by AI Messaging
cards.body: A WordPress VIP survey found that 60% of U.S. consumers view 'AI' in brand messaging as a turnoff. Consumers remain wary of AI-generated answers despite companies' investment in AI search.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO Coverage: Winners and What's Inside
cards.body: TechCrunch's comprehensive SpaceX IPO package covers the company's journey, major stakeholders, and regulatory filings. The coverage includes pre-IPO deals and analysis of long-term implications for the space industry.

cards.title: DOJ Backs xAI Gas Turbines on National Security Grounds
cards.body: The Justice Department argued that the Pentagon needs xAI's unpermitted gas turbines for national security. The DOJ framed the issue as critical to national, economic, and energy security.

cards.title: Plaud Surpasses $100M ARR With AI Notetakers
cards.body: Plaud announced its software business exceeded $100M in annual recurring revenue after shipping over 2M AI notetakers. The company is carving out space in an increasingly crowded meeting AI market.

cards.title: Robinhood Avoids AI Scapegoat in Layoff Announcement
cards.body: Robinhood's CEO conspicuously avoided blaming AI for the company's 10% workforce reduction. The move contrasts sharply with tech peers who cite AI restructuring as justification for mass layoffs.

cards.title: Probably Raises $9M to Build Reliable AI Systems
cards.body: Probably secured $9M to prevent hallucinations and factual errors in AI outputs. The startup aims to achieve accuracy matching deterministic systems, not traditional LLMs.

cards.title: Anthropic Joins Frontier Carbon Removal Coalition
cards.body: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received $915M in pledges for carbon removal projects. The move positions the AI startup among leaders addressing climate impact.

cards.title: World Models Next Big Thing After LLMs
cards.body: Odyssey, a world model startup, secured $1.45B valuation with backing from Amazon and other major investors. World models represent the next frontier in AI beyond large language models.

cards.title: Americans Skeptical About AI's Societal Impact
cards.body: Only 16% of Americans believe AI will positively impact society, according to Pew Research. The finding contrasts sharply with Wall Street's enthusiasm for the technology.

cards.title: Google Home Speaker Gets Gemini AI Upgrade
cards.body: Google's new $99.99 Home Speaker replaces rigid voice commands with conversational Gemini interactions. The company is betting generative AI can revitalize the smart speaker market.

cards.title: Robot Training Data Problem Demands Solutions
cards.body: Physical AI labs need massive amounts of training data to match LLM achievements. Some companies are already paying firms like XDOF to collect this critical data.

cards.title: Pramaana Labs Secures $27M for AI Verification
cards.body: Pramaana Labs raised $27M from Khosla Ventures to bring formal verification to AI systems. The startup targets high-stakes verticals like law, drug discovery, and tax preparation.

cards.title: DeepL Acquires Mixhalo for Live Translation
cards.body: DeepL acquired Mixhalo to expand its audio streaming and translation capabilities for live events. The move includes opening a new San Francisco office for U.S. expansion.

cards.title: Pinterest Launches Conversational AI Shopping App
cards.body: Pinterest's new 'Ask Pinterest' app uses AI to provide shopping recommendations through conversation. The experimental tool lets users seek inspiration in a more natural way.

cards.title: Anthropic's Popularity Grows Despite Government Tensions
cards.body: Anthropic's business popularity is surging among enterprise users despite recent conflicts with the Trump administration. Data from Ramp suggests the feud may actually boost adoption.

cards.title: Android 17 Expands Gemini AI Features
cards.body: Google released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with expanded Gemini AI capabilities across devices. New multitasking tools and security features accompany the latest Pixel Drop.

cards.title: Consumers Wary of AI in Brand Messaging
cards.body: 60% of US consumers find 'AI' in brand messaging a turnoff, according to WordPress VIP survey. The finding suggests disconnect between corporate AI enthusiasm and consumer sentiment.

cards.title: xAI's Gas Turbines Matter for National Security
cards.body: The Justice Department claims the Pentagon needs xAI's unpermitted gas turbines for national and energy security. The DOJ is arguing against litigation over the company's power infrastructure.

cards.title: Plaud AI Notetaker Business Hits $100M ARR
cards.body: Plaud surpassed $100M in annual recurring revenue after shipping over 2 million AI notetakers. The company competes in the crowded market for AI-powered meeting transcription.

cards.title: Probably Raises $9M to Prevent AI Hallucinations
cards.body: Probably secured $9M to build more reliable AI systems that avoid hallucinations and factual errors. The startup aims for accuracy on par with deterministic systems.

cards.title: Military Uses AI Models as Strategic Advisors
cards.body: MIT Technology Review's exclusive eBook explores how militaries deploy AI models for decision-making. Six updated stories examine the intersection of artificial intelligence and defense.

cards.title: Brain Implant User Becomes First AI Power User
cards.body: Casey Harrell, paralyzed with ALS, has become the first 'power user' of a brain-computer interface for speech. He's been using the implanted electrode system for nearly three years.

cards.title: South Koreans Lead Global AI Adoption Race
cards.body: South Korea has emerged as unusually enthusiastic about AI integration across society and government. The nation uses AI in immigration checkpoints and beyond.

cards.title: Dangerous AI Models Coming Regardless
cards.body: Experts warn that increasingly dangerous AI models will arrive despite regulatory efforts. The technology's capabilities are advancing faster than governance frameworks.

cards.title: Google Home Speaker Available for Preorder
cards.body: The $100 Google Home Speaker with Gemini AI is finally available for preorder after months of delay. The device replaces traditional voice assistant interactions with conversational AI.

cards.title: Anthropic Pauses Token-Based Claude SDK Billing
cards.body: Anthropic has paused token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK. The move addresses concerns about pricing structure for developers.

cards.title: Pentagon Uses AI to Write Congressional Reports
cards.body: The Pentagon confirmed it uses AI models to write reports mandated by Congress. The practice highlights increasing military reliance on AI assistance.

cards.title: Android 17 Hits Pixel Phones and Watches
cards.body: Android 17 began rolling out to Pixel devices with enhanced Gemini AI integration. The update includes new productivity and security features.

cards.title: SpaceX Acquires AI Coding Platform Cursor
cards.body: SpaceX announced a $60 billion acquisition of Cursor, an AI coding platform. The deal represents significant investment in AI-powered development tools.

cards.title: OpenAI Losing Billions Annually, Documents Show
cards.body: Leaked financial documents reveal OpenAI is losing billions of dollars per year. The losses underscore challenges in AI model training economics.

cards.title: Mobileye Enters US Robotaxi Market
cards.body: Intel's Mobileye is launching a standalone robotaxi service in the United States. The move positions the company as a direct competitor in autonomous vehicle transportation.
